problem: Development teams waste 40% of their time on context-switching between tools and re-explaining project context to AI assistants, resulting in inconsistent outputs and lost institutional knowledge.

target_users.goal: Ship features faster with AI assistance
target_users.frustration: AI assistants forget context between sessions
target_users.context: Daily coding sessions across multiple projects

target_users.frustration: Each team member gets different AI outputs for same requirements
target_users.context: Sprint planning and code review cycles

target_users.frustration: Specs drift from intent during implementation

competitors.name: Devin
competitors.our_advantage: Transparent recipes with explicit approval gates

market_size.som: $150M enterprise teams with structured dev processes
market_size.note: Estimates based on 2025 Gartner developer tools forecast

differentiators: Deterministic recipe execution with audit trails
Three-layer agent architecture for separation of concerns

risks.description: LLM cost per interaction may make the tool expensive for small teams
Enterprise adoption requires SOC2 compliance which is not yet achieved
Competing tools may adopt similar memory and workflow features

value_proposition: Meridian eliminates the context-switching tax in AI-assisted development by providing persistent project memory and deterministic workflows that produce consistent, auditable outputs across sessions and team members.

strategic_goals.title: Eliminate Context Loss
strategic_goals.description: AI assistants retain full project context across sessions
strategic_goals.measurement: Time from session start to first productive output

strategic_goals.title: Deterministic Workflows
strategic_goals.description: Same inputs produce consistent outputs regardless of who runs them
strategic_goals.measurement: Automated diff analysis of outputs from identical inputs

strategic_goals.title: Audit Trail Coverage
strategic_goals.description: Every design decision is traceable from intent to implementation
strategic_goals.target: 100% of architectural decisions linked to intent

success_metrics.measurement_method: Automated timing from session init to first tool call

assumptions: Teams have at least one technical member who can review AI outputs
LLM API costs will decrease 50% within 12 months
Enterprise customers will accept cloud-based LLM processing with SOC2 controls

out_of_scope.rationale: We orchestrate, not generate — code generation is delegated to underlying LLMs
We produce artifacts that feed into existing pipelines, not replace them
We complement PM tools like Linear/Jira, not replace them

product_profile.rationale: Integrates with git, GitHub, IDE — not deep SaaS integrations

nfr_profile.rationale: LLM latency dominates; local operations must be fast
Handles source code and design documents
CLI tool — failures are local, recoverable

quality_profile.rationale: Self-documenting system via ADRs and intent files
Framework code consumed by AI agents must be unambiguous
